title: Algebraic Structure, Equations, and Inequalities · Worked Case: Inequality Systems
summary: Build a tariff and break-even calculator with piecewise constraints. Planned artifact: Add and verify the math int inequality systems increment in an audited tariff and break-even model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Solve and graph compound and two-variable inequality systems as feasible regions.
Model thresholds and distance with absolute-value and piecewise relationships.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-intermediate cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

steps.title: Calculate Inequality Systems evidence
steps.instruction: A measured quantity starts at 21 units, changes by −8 units, then by +6 units. Calculate the final quantity.
steps.why: A numerical result is not complete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Preserve the signs and compute 21 − 8 + 6; compare with the starting scale.

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Predict the sign and approximate magnitude. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then compare it with that prediction.
